title: Generative AI Fundamentals
description: Understand the technology driving the AI revolution. Learn how Generative AI differs from traditional AI, explore the mechanics behind Large Language Models and Diffusion models, and examine the ethical implications of these technologies.

seo_metadata.title: Generative AI Fundamentals Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn the theory behind Generative AI. Understand LLMs, diffusion models, and the ethical impact of artificial intelligence.

learning_outcomes: Differentiate between Discriminative AI and Generative AI
Understand the basic architecture of Transformers and LLMs
Explain how Diffusion models generate images
Identify key ethical concerns, including bias and copyright issues
Evaluate the future impact of Generative AI on various industries

modules.title: Introduction to Generative AI
modules.description: What it is and how it differs from past technologies.

lessons.title: Discriminative vs. Generative
lessons.content:
A Paradigm Shift

Traditional (Discriminative) AI classifies data (e.g., 'Is this an image of a cat?'). Generative AI creates new data based on patterns it has learned (e.g., 'Draw a new image of a cat').

lessons.title: The Transformer Architecture
lessons.content:
The Breakthrough

The 'T' in ChatGPT stands for Transformer. Introduced in 2017, this architecture allows models to process entire sentences at once and understand the context of words based on their position, revolutionizing natural language processing.

modules.title: How the Models Work
modules.description: A high-level look under the hood.

lessons.title: Large Language Models (LLMs)
lessons.content:
Predicting the Next Word

At their core, LLMs are advanced statistical engines predicting the most probable next token (word piece) in a sequence, based on billions of parameters tuned during training.

lessons.title: Diffusion Models
lessons.content:
Creating Images from Noise

Image generators like DALL-E and Midjourney use Diffusion models. They are trained by adding static (noise) to an image until it is unrecognizable, then learning to reverse the process to generate a clear image from pure noise.

modules.title: Ethics, Risks, and the Future
modules.description: Navigating the complexities of AI adoption.

lessons.title: Bias and Hallucinations
lessons.content:
Flaws in the System

AI models inherit the biases present in their training data. Furthermore, they can 'hallucinate' facts because they prioritize statistical probability over factual accuracy.

lessons.title: Copyright and Security
lessons.content:
Uncharted Territory

Generative AI raises massive questions about copyright infringement (who owns AI art?) and data security (should you paste proprietary company code into ChatGPT?).

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between Discriminative AI and Generative AI?

options.content: Discriminative AI is faster
Discriminative AI classifies existing data; Generative AI creates new data
Generative AI only works with images
There is no difference

mcqs.question: What does the 'T' in ChatGPT stand for?

options.content: Text
Translation
Transformer
Technology

mcqs.question: At a fundamental level, how does a Large Language Model (LLM) generate text?

options.content: By searching a database of pre-written answers
By predicting the most probable next token (word) in a sequence
By translating code into English
By asking a human supervisor

mcqs.question: Which type of model is primarily used by modern AI image generators like Midjourney?

options.content: Large Language Models
Decision Trees
Diffusion Models
Linear Regression

mcqs.question: How do Diffusion models learn to generate images?

options.content: By learning to reverse the process of adding static (noise) to an image
By copying existing images from Google
By reading descriptions of colors
By predicting the next pixel in a line

mcqs.question: Why do AI models sometimes exhibit bias?

options.content: Because they have personal opinions
Because the programmers intentionally code bias into them
Because they inherit the biases present in the massive datasets they are trained on
Because they are not trained enough
When the AI generates an image of a ghost
When the AI confidently presents incorrect or fabricated information as fact
When the AI shuts down due to overheating
When the AI refuses to answer a prompt

mcqs.question: Why is pasting proprietary company data into public AI models like ChatGPT considered a security risk?

options.content: Because the AI might use that data for future training, exposing it to others
Because the AI will delete the data from your computer
Because the AI will email your boss
It is not a security risk

mcqs.question: What breakthrough did the Transformer architecture bring to natural language processing?

options.content: The ability to process audio files natively
The ability to process words sequentially, one by one
The ability to process entire sequences at once and understand context via 'attention'
The ability to generate video from text

mcqs.question: What is a major unresolved legal issue surrounding Generative AI?

options.content: Whether AI should have a passport
Copyright infringement regarding the data used to train the models and the ownership of the output
Whether AI can vote
Taxation of AI models
